title: Midterm Examination — English Grammar (M.1)
description: Covers grammar, error identification, reading comprehension, and vocabulary.

sections.title: Grammar Multiple Choice
sections.instructions: Directions: Choose the best answer to complete each sentence.

questions.question: Yesterday, my father ______ home late from work.
questions.explanation: 'Yesterday' signals past simple; the past form of 'come' is 'came'.

questions.question: You ______ finish your homework before you play games.
This exercise is ______ than the last one.
______ is your birthday? — It's in June.
There isn't ______ milk in the fridge.
questions.explanation: 'Much' is used with uncountable nouns like 'milk' in negatives.

sections.title: Grammar in Context
sections.instructions: Directions: Read the paragraph and choose the correct word for each blank.

passages.title: My Weekend
passages.passage: Last Saturday, I ___(6)___ to the park with my family. The weather ___(7)___ beautiful, and there ___(8)___ many people enjoying the sunshine. We had a picnic under a big tree and played frisbee for hours.

questions.question: Blank (6): I ______ to the park.
Blank (7): The weather ______ beautiful.
Blank (8): There ______ many people.

sections.title: Error Identification
sections.instructions: Directions: Choose the underlined part that is grammatically incorrect.

questions.question: She (A) has went (B) to the (C) library yesterday (D).
We (A) are waiting (B) for the bus (C) since (D) 8 o'clock.
questions.explanation: With 'since', use present perfect continuous: 'have been waiting'.

questions.question: There are (A) too (B) much (C) students (D) in the classroom.

sections.title: Reading Comprehension
sections.instructions: Directions: Read the passage and answer the questions that follow. Questions 12–15 refer to this passage.

passages.title: The School Library
passages.passage:
Our school library is a quiet and comfortable place. It opens from Monday to Friday between 8 a.m. and 4 p.m. Students can borrow up to three books at one time and keep them for one week. The library has over five thousand books in both Thai and English, as well as computers with free internet for research. Ms. Somsri, the librarian, is always happy to help students find the books they need. Every Wednesday afternoon, the library holds a reading club where students share their favourite stories.

Students must be quiet inside the library. Food and drinks are not allowed. Students who lose or damage a book must pay to replace it.

questions.question: How many books can a student borrow at one time?
questions.explanation: The passage says 'up to three books at one time'.

questions.question: When does the reading club meet?
questions.explanation: 'Every Wednesday afternoon, the library holds a reading club'.

questions.question: Which action is NOT allowed in the library?
The word 'damage' in the last paragraph is closest in meaning to ______ .
questions.explanation: 'Damage' means to harm or break something.

sections.title: Vocabulary
sections.instructions: Directions: Choose the word that best completes each sentence.

questions.question: My grandfather is very ______ ; he always tells the truth.
questions.explanation: Someone who tells the truth is honest.

questions.question: Please ______ a photo of us before we leave.
What is the opposite of 'brave'?
The word 'huge' means almost the same as ______ .
Her ______ in the competition surprised everyone. (success)
questions.explanation: A noun is needed after 'Her'; the noun form is 'success'.
